end of world model.

Level 1: you have used 51 actions; a strong score needs about 20 or fewer. You are 2.5x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 51 actions returned the board to a state already seen 51 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 51 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.

</function>

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game is over, will auto-reset. Let's check current state and try new actions.
# The game should auto-reset on next turn, but let's try clicking cross centers now.
print(f"State: {last_action_result.get('state')}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

# Try clicking black center of top-right cross at [10, 52]
print("\nTry clicking black center [10, 52]")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 52}])
print(f"Result: score={last_action_result.get('score')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

if not last_action_result.get('game_over') and not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    # Try clicking black center of bottom-left cross at [52, 10]
    print("\nTry clicking black center [52, 10]")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 10}])
    print(f"Result: score={last_action_result.get('score')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
